topic: Two people discover they've been working on identical projects
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the line of the schematic with a trembling finger. The faint, almost imperceptible smudge of graphite on the blueprint matched the one she had meticulously applied just an hour prior. A cold dread began to pool in her stomach, tightening her chest until breathing became shallow. She looked up, her eyes wide, to the adjacent workstation. There, sitting across the room, was a figure hunched over the exact same diagram, their brow furrowed in concentration. A sharp intake of breath escaped her lips, and she instinctively recoiled, knocking over a stack of reference manuals. "No," she whispered, the sound brittle in the quiet lab.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the monitor, his knuckles white where he gripped the edge of the desk. The progress bar on the simulation software displayed the identical sequence of operations they had both coded, line by painstaking line. A wave of profound unease washed over him, making his hands shake uncontrollably. He slowly turned his head, his gaze locking onto the other occupant of the space. The other person was mirroring his posture, the same slight tension around the jaw. Mark pushed back from his chair, the scrape of metal on tile echoing loudly in the silence. "This isn't possible," he managed, his voice thin and strained.

[story 3]

When Anya finally looked up, the realization hit her like a physical blow. The meticulously organized notes on the whiteboard, the specific, idiosyncratic notation she had scrawled down earlier, were reproduced in perfect fidelity on the board directly beside her. Her breath hitched, a sudden, sharp constriction in her throat. She stood up too quickly, stumbling slightly, her gaze darting from the identical hardware setup to the person seated opposite her. Her mouth opened, but no sound emerged; only a slight, involuntary tremor ran through her hands as she gripped the edge of the table, unable to process the uncanny symmetry before her.
